My eyes scan left and right looking for anything recognizable. A small panic fills me, its only been a year, why cant i find my path. Then as quickly as fear enters me it fades away. The trees line up and show me a long forgotten road. Many fallen branches and trees lay over it. I often wonder if the bigger trees where cut down to block this road specifically. Just tress i would have to clear at some point in the forever future in my mind. Its on a small incline up as well. Witch makes me out of breath faster then i would like. So i stop half way up the road. My excitement has only made me go too fast.

On this abandoned road there are hills that go up on either side. Both have white rocks randomly peppered along them. Quarts is what Asa tells me the rocks are. They are like trapped pockets of white light they you can just happen to pick up. They are from the dynamite i assume. This place is a old rock quarry, where they got the marital for the dam. Signs are randomly placed telling you to keep away. un-exploded dynamite supposedly still exists. Its been so many years and i have seen/heard of no explosions yet. Ive stood here too long, my eagerness gets the best of me.

About five more minuets up this road then to the left over a small hill. That is when you see it. I dont know if it is just appeling to me or if it is magnificent to everyone. As you look out you see a small clearing with what looks to me a marshy area. Bright green thick grass grows out of it. A little bit past that is young trees trying to grow. Some are only a few years new and others stand fifteen feet tall. To the left and right are cliff faces. Man made from all the dynamite explosions. The one to the left is almost too perfectly straight up and down. It has the color of earth, red with a few tan looking parts. Loose rocks from the size of bolders on down are tossed around at the bottom of these cliffs.

This is my favorite place. Because it is a valley like this i feel protected/held. The first time i came i explored the tops of both cliffs and around them. Now every time i come i get to the bottom of the vally, amoung the teen age trees and feel at peace. I spend the time thinking about the small log cabin i want to build here. Where my garden will be and how long the walk will be to the river for fishing. If i would be able to dig out a cellar. Its all planned out in my head. I have a spot that i have made there. It looks like a grave but to me it is a spot. It is a seat, surrounded by smallish rocks with the bottom filled in with new pine needles every time i come. It is incredibly comfortable and the view is one i long to see every day.

I want to see the sun rise across the small view of the sky that you can see from the valley. I want to fill the cliff face with laughter and shadow puppets. I want to have a dog that goes with me everywhere unbounded by leashes. I want to know every inch of the area like the back of my hand. A lot of wants, right? To many? I don't think so. Everyone has there preference and dream place. Or i assume everyone does. If not what do people day dream about? The want is negated by the simple fact that im ok with waiting for this. That might be what makes it even better. I get to keep dreaming about it, once every few years going back to make my memories of the area even more vivid.

-Tom

title: Deterioration
text:
I am taking this time to explain why i am a failure. This is not necessarily a bad thing. Often you will hear influential people explain that failures make us who we are. Well in that case at some point in time, i will be super great. Today though, not great. Abysmally bad. This also is not happening all the sudden. Its taken years. In fact i don’t even know if i ever was successful. I have a hard time ever thinking i was.

It all started when i was a kid and things came ever so slightly to easily for me. It made me think i could coast through life without trying and be successful. As we all know, that is not how the real world works. When i started home schooling i came to a standstill and something that should have taken me a year or two tops took three or four. Only finishing because i felt bad about how much money my mom had spent to have me home school.

When i started home schooling i got a part time job. This job gave me close to six hundred dollars a month. For fifteen year old me that was a kings wealth. So on my off days and weekends i would take my car to the city and goof off. I did nothing productive with my life. Like learning a trade or useful skill. I thought i could coast, that it would all work out. Things come easy to me, remember. I was so incredibly wrong.

I've moved away from my parents house twice. Coming back both times because i couldn’t handle life. I always had excuses for moving home the first time. Looking back on it now with fresh eyes i can see that it was my failure to cope with life. The second time i moved home, that i knew exactly what was happening.

I struggle to know when im being terrible to my self because of my brains chemical imbalances and when i am just terrible at life naturally. This is because my brain is almost constantly telling me that i am a worthless waist of space that shouldn’t exist. Now i know that everyone gets this way sometimes. So i thought it was completely normal. It turns out that it should be a brief passing thought. Not a pervasive idea that shapes everything in your world view. This type of thought pattern also often leads to anxity for witch i also thought was completly normal. Turns out, not the case. (If you get bored and want to read about major depressive disorder go

here!

)

I don’t want to use it as a crutch though. I have traits that don’t work. I am quite lazy. I give up incredibly fast. I bore easily. I can be judgmental and rude. These are things that most people find ways to work through. I too date have none of them handled. They are worsened by my brains self loathing. Most days i set up one task for me to do. Do laundry. Wash dishes. Clean Bathroom. Mail a letter. Buy groceries. These are things that most of the world accomplishes in one day easily. They seem like giant mountains to me. Often even though its just one simple task i fail to do anything. Encased in sloth like anxiety and self doubt.

I live in the sentience “whats the point.” When everything looks like a cliff face sheering off into a black pit of nothingness then having fresh clothes or washed dishes seems like, well pointless. So most of my days i spend distracting my self. Reading, listening and watching my self into a coma. This is the only time that my brain settles down for a bit. It stays quiet from the relentless beat down.

So when the only time you feel slightly ok is when you are consuming content you fall into a routine. My daily tasks get ignored. Work feels like a endless drag that can only be fixed by getting home and not thinking any more. I am self medicating my self by ignoring my problems. I am wasting my life in this process. No marriageable woman would ever look at me and see a provider or a useful companion. I don’t blame them. That last bit is just a dig at my self. Ignore it. Back to the point at hand.

I am a failure.

cool.

title: Static
text:
I have hit a new point in my never ending spiral towards madness. For years when I had a depressive week it felt like a knife slowly carving out my heart. It was a dull aching pain that pulsed slowly. Sometimes fading into the back ground to be forgotten but always coming back. It was at these moments highs and lows that I was mostly active in life. Because I still felt something, I still had internal pain that in some odd way kept me going.

These last few months though, something new has happened. The best way I can describe it is static. Where my life before was hills and valleys this is like a flat path. But fill the path with sound proof foam and roll me up into it. No longer do I feel much of anything any more. I still feel the need to use the bathroom, eat and sleep. Everything else is just a low hiss in the background.

For a while I thought it was just a valley that I would come out of, but as time moves forward nothing changes. In some ways its a good thing. I feel less unstable, more sure of my sanity. In other ways it is detrimental. Things that I hold valuable and things that bring me joy look gray. Nothing holds my interest or thought anymore. Because of that i have become even less active in my own life. I have withdrawn more.

It makes me so worried that I will never come out of it. Spending time with friends something that in the past always could snap me out of anything. Now it is just a waiting game. Just me waiting till the obligation to smile, laugh and act like a functional human being is over. I understand how awful that is to say. I also wish I knew how to take the mask off and show how I truly feel.

Something about my personality or the way I was raised caused me to be entertaining and a people person. I have no clue how to stop it. If I just sit around quietly people just ask me whats wrong. Of course something is wrong but no one wants to hear anyones issues. My mind knows that if I am a bummer all the time I will loose everyone. So it puts on a show. The only time I don’t feel tension is when I am alone. When with people I’m burning special fuel that I don’t have much off. Its why I often plan days of nothing after days of seeing friends. To rest and recharge, to get over the fact that i cant feel for them the way i used too.

From what I have read this is the last stage. Nothing much more happens after this. You either find help or you stay in the static. I dont know how long I can stay here. Right now there is a odd comforting nature about it. For months I have needed to call one number to set up a doctors appointment. For months I have done nothing. Why cant I just call? Have I grown accustom to being nothing. Am I scared of how much a feeling mind will hate what I have become? Change is hard. Thats what it is. I will call. I have to call.

-Tom

I keep trying to write a ‘what i want 2016 to be blog’. I get about two hundred words into it and i just get sad. The things i want and the thing that happen always follow such different paths. I start to think of me sitting down the last day of 2016 and reading about all my dreams and wants. All these things that are so attainable, so reasonable. I see my self crying for hours. Nothing was accomplished. wait. Nothing will be accomplished.

When i think about 2015 i only see it as a year of decline. It started off alright, i had January off for mental health. What did i do during January? Nothing. Then i went on a giant trip to my favorite place in the world with all my friends. That was the highlight of my year. Only because its simple and clean. Its friends, trees and fires. My brother got engaged witch led to march? or may? Going to Portland for the wedding. Another high point in my year.

I cant think of any more notable moments that i felt normal. The rest of the year flew past me. I was asleep. Or my brain failed to register it.

This is awful. this is why i dont write anymore. its always awful.

title: One of the sum.
text:
One of the sum.

Lately i feel like a homebody. Whats that word for someone who doesn’t go outside? Agoraphobia. Now that i just googled it sadly it seems to fit me. Its always amazing that nothing is unique. Just lots of people dealing with similar circumstances. We are all just trying to survive. A sum of the whole. Tons of little things or events shape the way we are. How we react to situations and how we choose to live our collective life. Im here to tell you about one of my things. Something that shaped me in hopes of you understanding me slightly better.

What im talking about is psoriasis. Google it if you want but basically ‘A condition in which skin cells build up and form scales and itchy, dry patches.” and it affects 2%-4% of the human population. In history it has been called leprosy wrongly but back then they didn't know better. Im just going to quote the Wikipedia page on it. “Individuals with psoriasis may feel self-conscious about their appearance and have a poor self-image that stems from fear of public rejection and psychosexual concerns. Psoriasis has been associated with low self-esteem and

depression

is more common among those with the condition.

[4]

People with psoriasis often feel prejudiced against due to the commonly held incorrect belief that psoriasis is contagious.

[44]

Psychological distress can lead to significant

depression

and

social isolation

; a high rate of

thoughts about suicide

has been associated with psoriasis.”

Ok so now that you slightly know about it, let me tell you my experience. I think it was around puberty that in noticed something strange on my scalp. So im thinking 13–14 years old. It started as just a small spot but over time spread. For much of my youth it only ever existed on my scalp. It slowly moved down to my forehead though. Thats why if you look at photos of me in my youth i had long bangs that came down to my eyebrows that i kept neatly brushed forward to hide my forehead. It was something that was always on my mind. If someone could see it, what they would think.

It is constantly itchy and being on my scalp i often end up scratching or running my hands through my hair. Trying anything i can to calm it slightly so i can live for one second without the constant alarms of itch in my mind. I remember having a cold or something and going to see a doctor. I had to have been 15 or 16 years old. I figured this was my time to have a professional look at it. I remember so vividly her saying “hmm i have never seen this before…” Great. Super cool. She prescribed me a bottle of shampoo that cost my mom like 60 dollars. It did nothing.

The thing about psoriasis is that it moves around. Eventually it left my forehead. Moved to the sides of my scalp. To my elbows and to random other spots on my body. Its a constant cause of concern to me. I have tried so many different things to try to calm it or heal it. Yes most likely what you are thinking to tell me to try right now, i have tried. My next step is to get a UVB lamp and try light therapy. Its not going to cure it and i will have to do it for the rest of my life but it beats some of the medications they will put you on.

I wonder what my life would be like. No what i would be like if i never had psoriasis. Would i be less afraid of what people think of me. More confidant, more mental power to put to other things. I know it had affected me negatively. How deeply it affects me? Im not sure. Can i blame my depression and anxiety on it? I do, for parts of it. As i said at the begining though, we are a sum of the whole. What ifs never do any good. I am what i will be and i have accepted that.

Im writing this now mostly because at the moment it has moved to my ears and neck. Encroaching on my face. The things you cant do with psoriasis is cause friction or cuts to happen. It will only cause it to spread faster. So at the moment i have chosen to grow a beard. In theory it will protect me from it spreading to my face. In practice it is also helping to hide the bit that already has gotten to my face.

Obviously with my agoraphobic nature currently it has not been much of a issue. I only see a few people and they understand whats happening. At work no one even bats a eye as beards are quite common currently. The thing i struggle with currently is going places where i know the people but they dont know what is going on with me. I wish i could live in a world where people just knew what was up. Im hoping this blog post informs. In reality though, not many people read this.

So soon, i am going to be trying to expand my boundary's that i have set so small currently. I will be seeing a doctor soon and getting treatment for my depression and anxiety hopefully. I wish to return to the world a slightly whole person. So if you see me and my beard. Im ok…or well im as ok as i can be. Please dont tell me about what will cure my incurable genetic autoimmune disease.

I just want a nice conversation about positive things.

Thats all. I think thats all any of us wants.

If you have made it this far, thank you so much for reading. I appreciate the interest you are showing in me.

-Tom

title: Deepest Desires
text:
I have only ever owned one dog. Her name was Ranger and she was the best thing I have ever known. When I close my eyes I still see her brown eyes open wide, staring back at me with pupils exuding more love than I knew existed. Ranger was always there for me. If you don’t know the love of a dog, it’s a love more pure and innocent and simple and strong than any other. If you don’t know the love of a dog, I am sorry. There’s no sadness in the love of a dog. But then again, nothing is sad until it’s over. Then everything is.

I don’t know what it is that makes me a dog person. All I know is that I have always loved dogs and I make happy sounds the moment I see one. Something about them fits me — their willingness to love, their need for approval — I don’t know. My brain is sick. It’s broken like so many backyard swing sets of neglectful kids, needing so much the care of a dad and a can of oil. It squeaks and groans with every passing moment and every stray gust of wind. Every squeak, a negative thought about myself reminding me how broken I am. She helped me fight that. Ranger was always there for me. Every day as I struggled to get out of bed, she was there. She was there with those same soft brown eyes reminding me that something in this world still loved me. Cuddling with her in bed and napping made her day. It made my day too. Every time she wanted to be with me was ammunition to use against my own mind. Every time it told me how awful I was, I could return volley with a memory of Ranger wanting to be with me. She gave me evidence that my mind couldn’t be entirely right. She always wanted to be with me, even when I didn’t.

Having a responsibility, a living thing that depended upon me to survive, was good for me. She had food costs and vet bills that I had to factor into my life. When earning money to feed myself wasn’t motivating, knowing that she needed me was. Every morning she would wake me up because she needed me to take her outside. She was the routine that I so craved.

When I had Ranger, I was stable. I wanted to move forward and join Swahili in Fort Worth. I made some fast plans, taking advantage of friendships in the city, and moved. The first place I landed was in a brother’s house with my friend, Lonzo. He didn’t like pets and didn’t have a back yard, which meant that Ranger wasn’t welcome. I was so blinded by my ambition to do Swahili that I didn’t think much of not having her. Being in that house allowed me to be with one of my closest friends, to use him to hold me up, to help fend of the wolves hiding in my neurons. Ranger was always there for me. I wasn’t there for her.

Time washed on. Lonzo got married and moved out. The other brother wanted to sell his house. I moved into an apartment with my cousin, Seth. He brought his dog — I didn’t think bringing ranger would have worked out. My parents started to look for a new home for Ranger. She was spending day after day in their backyard, alone. It wasn’t fair to her. My parents, gracious in their agreement to take care of her this long, weren’t in a position to do it any longer. They found her a new home and a new family. I hope she loves them as much as she loved me.

Seth got married and moved out. Faced with an empty house again the termites ate away at my brain’s supports. Within months I crumbled.

At this moment I don’t think I will ever be able to hold myself up. I don’t like that fact. I try to convince myself otherwise but so far it is true. It’s only in looking back that I see how important Ranger was for me. She gave me all the building blocks I needed to let me have a go at a normal life. The kind of life everyone else seems to get with so little effort.

There is also a large chance that back then my depression and anxiety weren’t as bad as they are now. Maybe I am just fitting her in to explain my successful years. Maybe I’m finding patterns where none actually exist. I don’t care. She did help me become a better person — even if only in my mind. Then again, what part of this isn’t in my mind? I was too headstrong and foolish to make room in my life for her. It’s something I will forever regret. All these memories are on my mind because of Sailor. Sailor was Braden and Rachel’s lovely dog. I’ve dog sit for them a few times and she was the closest now to what Ranger was to me then.

Sailor was a giant lovable oaf that just wanted to cuddle and chase sticks. She knew plenty of tricks but would only ever perform them for new people. I’ve often said that she could understand English. You could tell her what you wanted her to do and she would do exactly that. Even more amazing is how much Braden and Rachel loved her. You could tell how excited Sailor is by her bunny hops. Every time she saw them, she hopped and hopped and hopped. Sailor made me remember in a way I thought I could again.

Sailor had cancer. After battling for a while, they lovingly put her down. I miss her so much for me and for them.

I’m trying to beat my brain back into place. I am seeing a doctor and on am on medication. It’s not going well. I sleep most of the day. When I’m awake there is only a flood of negative thoughts. I isolate myself more and more because I think i will only disappoint the people I see. I’m thankful to have loving parents who let me stay in their home. At twenty-seven I worry about my future. Will I always need people to hold me up? It’s my greatest desire in the world to get another dog. My parents, who have been selfless and wonderful throughout this all, don’t want a inside dog. In a normal person’s life that isn’t a problem. They would just save up and find a place to live and go on with their dreams. But here I am. Incapable of accomplishing that, feeling constantly more negative for being incapable. I live in a circle of despair. Here I am, writing a thousand words on how miserable my easy American life is. I am ridiculous. My brain mocking me.

-Tom

title: Static — part ll
text:
Static — part ll

In my last post about my mental health i talked about how i felt like i was in static. Its been a while since then and i have been meaning to write a update, so here it is. Since then i have started to see a doctor once every two months. After evaluation i was found to have major depressive disorder and general anxiety disorder. I was started on some SSRI’s to try and help with these two conditions. With the first medication i could feel it helping but it also made me so tired that some days i was sleeping 18+ hours. That was like not being alive anyway so i talked to my doctor at my next visit.

I was then given Prozac, the poster pill for depression. It takes time to work, changing the chemistry of your brain. I now take three pills every night. I hate the taste of them in my mouth, so i try to down them fast. What is it like? For me its like static, but a different kind. I can feel it slow down my thought pattern, it causes me to loose focus more often. It still causes me to sleep more then normal but i cant accept that. My only side effects are that i sweat more and yawn more.

So with the depression before the medication my thought patterns were often dark and negative. They grew at times too, sometimes so heavy that i would think that death was the only escape. These days i still have some negative thoughts, but they are something i can only expect to have a few days a week instead of almost constantly. The thought that death would somehow be a escape seems almost foreign to me. I sometimes miss my fast thinking brain, but i will give it up any day for my more positive outlook on life.

This medication is also to help with my anxiety. To some extent it does, with my slowed thought patterns its easier to not spiral into a panic attack. Other then that though i still experience all the same panics that i always have. I dont think there is a drug way out of anxiety. I think you have to find your triggers and fight them. So thats what im doing, trying to think less of what could happen and more about being in the moment. Not worrying about who will be at the grocery store and what i would need to say or do.

With all this progress though, there hasn't been much progress. My doctor has told me it will take time to get back to whatever my “normal” life was. So while im feeling better most days, i still hide at my house. Its been a while since i have done normal things. So i like i pretend im a bear coming out of hibernation, got to take it slow. Find some cool berries to eat.

I just hope it doesn't take too long.

~Tom
